event: mild severe acute coronavirus respiratory virus-2 (SARS-COV-2) two months prior
left lower extremity deep vein thrombosis
wet gangrene of left fourth digit
X-ray left hand showed atrophy of fourth digit with subcutaneous gas
CT abdomen/pelvis revealed consolidative and reticular opacities in right lower lung
CT chest showed cavitary right lower lobe lesion with possible reversed halo sign
sepsis from suspected skin/soft tissue infection
concern for necrotizing hand infection from gas-producing organism
no viable tissue observed throughout hand
pending infectious studies (bacterial, fungal, mycobacterial cultures)
infiltrates of right middle and lower lobe
diffuse necrotic tissue past right lower lobe bronchus with fibrinous clot
MRI brain showed bilateral frontal lobe punctate infarcts
TEE showed 1.6 cm right atrial vegetation
urgent AngioVac extraction of right atrial vegetation
no tissue obtained for culture or pathology
bronchoalveolar lavage cytologic specimen showed pauciseptate hyphae
right lower lobe surgical specimen showed pauciseptate hyphae
left hand surgical specimen showed pauciseptate hyphae
hyphae present within vessels and around nerves
re-admitted to ICU for hypotension requiring vasopressors
